name: Register-EngineEvent

options.name: -Action
options.description: Specifies commands to handle the events. The commands in the Action run when an event is raised, instead of sending the event to the event queue. Enclose the commands in braces (`{}`) to create a script block.

options.name: -Forward
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet sends events for this subscription to the session on the local computer. Use this parameter when you are registering for events on a remote computer or in a remote session.

options.name: -MaxTriggerCount
options.description: Specifies the maximum number of times that the action is executed for the event subscription.

options.name: -MessageData
options.description: Specifies additional data associated with the event. The value of this parameter appears in the MessageData property of the event object.

options.name: -SourceIdentifier
options.description: Specifies the source identifier of the event to which you are subscribing. The source identifier must be unique in the current session. This parameter is required.

options.name: -SupportEvent
options.description: Indicates that the cmdlet hides the event subscription. Add this parameter when the current subscription is part of a more complex event registration mechanism and it should not be discovered independently.

description: Subscribes to events that are generated by the PowerShell engine and by the `New-Event` cmdlet.
